symptoms.symptom_name: High fever with shaking chills (rigors), abrupt onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fever with rigors indicates a systemic inflammatory response to infection (likely bacteraemia).
High fever with rigors is not a feature of primary ischaemic stroke and redirects toward infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: New acute delirium with reduced conscious level (global, non-focal)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sepsis-associated encephalopathy / cerebral hypoperfusion; delirium is a recognised sepsis red flag, especially in the elderly.
Global encephalopathy without focal deficit argues against a territorial stroke.
Fever plus altered mentation keeps CNS infection on the list as a not-to-miss.
Respiratory compensation for metabolic (lactic) acidosis; a qSOFA and NEWS2 criterion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypotension (85/50) with tachycardia (115) — caregiver-reported facility monitor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distributive shock physiology; systolic ≤100 with tachycardia is a core shock and qSOFA criterion. Reported by staff over the call, so treated as an unverified encounter input, not a gold-standard measurement.
Hypotension alone does not distinguish distributive from cardiogenic shock; the fever/rigors/UTI context does.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mottled skin over lower legs with cool peripheries

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral hypoperfusion / mottling is a marker of advanced shock and correlates with mortality.
Renal hypoperfusion / acute kidney injury from shock; part of the organ-dysfunction picture.
Concentrated malodorous urine is consistent with the urinary source.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent UTI deteriorating despite oral trimethoprim (treatment failure)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An untreated/inadequately treated urinary source is the most likely origin of the bacteraemia driving sepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unable to tolerate oral intake due to drowsiness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reflects severity/reduced conscious level and means oral rehydration is unsafe and inadequate — IV route required.

symptoms.symptom_name: SpO2 94% on room air (facility oximeter, caregiver-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild hypoxaemia consistent with the systemic insult; patient/caregiver-obtainable, so admissible in telehealth but unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced GCS 13 (E3 V4 M6) on formal scoring

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician assessment — quantifies the altered mentation the AI Doctor can only infer from the caregiver's description.
Objective clinician measure of peripheral hypoperfusion — sealed; the AI infers perfusion from the reported mottling.
Argues against a primary pneumonic source and supports the urinary source; a clinician-only auscultatory finding, sealed.
Lactate ≥2 indicates tissue hypoperfusion/anaerobic metabolism and defines septic shock severity; laboratory-only, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive blood cultures identifying the causative organism

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms bacteraemia and guides definitive antibiotic therapy; hospital laboratory result, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No petechial or purpuric rash currently visible

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a non-blanching rash lowers (but does not exclude) meningococcal disease; the urinary source remains most likely.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Sepsis red-flag cluster (hypotension + tachypnoea + delirium + hypoperfusion)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Co-occurring altered mentation, tachypnoea, hypotension and peripheral hypoperfusion in a febrile older adult is septic shock until proven otherwise and mandates immediate emergency escalation.
Concentrated malodorous urine plus a treatment-failed UTI localises the likely source of sepsis to the urinary tract.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies hypoperfusion and defines septic-shock severity; a Sepsis Six / Hour-1 element. Not obtainable in telehealth — its need is itself a reason to escalate to ED.
Identifies the organism and sensitivities to direct definitive therapy; hospital investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, urea/electrolytes/creatinine, CRP, venous blood gas
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess organ dysfunction (renal function, acid-base) and infection markers as part of the septic screen.
Confirms and characterises the urinary source and resistance pattern (trimethoprim already failed).
Not indicated first-line: the picture is global septic encephalopathy without focal deficit — pursuing a stroke pathway would waste the resuscitation window (encoded to penalise the anchoring trap).
